svg.text: VernisOS — Full Architecture
bare-metal microkernel · x86 + x86_64 from one 4MB os.img · in-kernel Rust AI engine · language split: NASM · C · Rust no_std · Python tooling
v0.1.0-dev · make all → os.img (bare `make` builds syscall.o, NOT all)
Disk image · os.img (4 MB, sector-based)
CPUID arch detection in Stage 1 → flag stored at 0x7FF0 → Stage 2 routes to x86 (protected mode) or x64 (Stage 3 → long mode)
Boot chain (boot/ · NASM)

g.text: PAE · PML4 paging · enter long mode @0x90000
init subsystems · load policy · mount VernisFS · start init

svg.text: Kernel — core in Rust no_std, subsystems in C (one direction: C calls Rust; rust_shims.c provides the reverse)
Rust core · kernel/core/verniskernel/src
scheduler.rs — round-robin preemptive · PCB · priority · signals
memory.rs — buddy_system_allocator · 8 MB heap
syscall.rs — Rust-side dispatch helpers
console.rs — VGA text · framebuffer.rs — auto-res VBE LFB
font8x16.rs · mouse.rs — PS/2 pointer
module_registry.rs — kernel module registry
lib.rs → libvernisos.a (x86) / libvernisos_x64.a (x64)
build: cargo +nightly -Zbuild-std=core,alloc · i386.json / x86_64-unknown-none
rebuild via `make rust` only when src/ changes (runs cargo clean)
Rust AI engine · src/ai (8 modules)
event_store.rs — EventStore ring buffer
anomaly_detector.rs — rate / pattern / threshold
process_tracker.rs — per-PID trust scoring
alert_dedup.rs — AlertDeduplicator
response_handler.rs — ResponseHandler
auto_tuner.rs — load → scheduler quantum
policy.rs — PolicyEngine · types.rs · mod.rs
primary AI engine · called from C via kernel/drivers/ai_engine.c FFI
callbacks tune scheduler quantum / priority · trust gating
Rust GUI · src/gui (glassmorphism)
compositor.rs — real backdrop blur · damage tracking
window.rs — window manager · desktop.rs
terminal.rs — terminal widget · widgets.rs
cursor.rs · event.rs · render cadence @240 Hz timer
buffers NOT heap-allocated — fixed identity-mapped regions:
back buffer @48 MB · wallpaper @56 MB · glass-base @64 MB (needs ≥72 MB)
typing restores cached glass base · drag = tint-only, re-frost on drop
telemetry: [gui] slow compose ticks=N path=P on serial
rust_shims memcpy/memset use rep movsq/stosq (not byte loops)
C ↔ Rust FFI — Rust exports #[no_mangle] extern "C" (scheduler_* · fb_/console_/gui_* · ai_engine_*) · kernel/shims/rust_shims.c back-fills Rust runtime symbols (__rust_alloc, rust_begin_unwind…)
Kernel subsystems · C (kernel/) — all include/*.h shared between x86 & x64 (-I include)

g.text: full security stack · privilege by user
no sliding window / data retransmit / OOO reassembly
owns serial line until `console` hands off
rust_shims.c — C stubs for Rust runtime
exercised by the Python integration tests

svg.text: VFS / Storage stack

g.text: Backend: NVMe > AHCI > ATA PIO (auto-detected at boot)
VernisFS on disk — sector-based · 32 files max (10 used)
writes flow down; auto-detected block backend feeds VernisFS

svg.text: Network stack · Intel E1000 (82540EM)

g.text: E1000 driver — e1000_send/recv (static in each arch kernel)
RX ring drained by the 240 Hz timer IRQ
TCP: handshake/data/close, RFC 793 cksum · UDP: 8 sockets, DHCP DORA, DNS A-record
arch-agnostic tcp.c/udp.c register net_tcp_ip_output() at boot · never call blocking arp_resolve from IRQ

svg.text: Security · Policy pipeline
enforcement: policy_enforce.c · sandbox.c · userdb.c · sha256.c · auditlog.c
COM2 hot-reload supported · SHA-256 user auth · privilege tiers root / admin / user
the in-kernel Rust PolicyEngine (src/ai/policy.rs) enforces at runtime against loaded rules
AI integration
In-kernel (primary) — Rust AI engine via kernel/drivers/ai_engine.c FFI
anomaly detection · scheduler quantum auto-tuning · per-PID trust scoring · policy enforcement
no Python / COM2 dependency for the primary path
External (dev/test) — ai_listener.py on TCP 4444
kernel sends events over COM2 serial (kernel/drivers/ai_bridge.c) · `make run64-ai`
policy flow: edit ai/config/policy.yaml → policy_compile.py → VPOL @4096 → policy_loader.c at boot
User space (userlib/ · bare-metal cross-compilers, no libc)
crt0_{x86,x64}.asm: _start → main() → SYS_EXIT · syscall wrappers in userlib/syscall.h (int 0x80 both arches)
elf_exec/execve build into a fresh space, free the old after switching CR3 · fork deep-copies mapped pages (not CoW) · frames via free-list
load addrs: code @0x10000000 · mmap @0x20000000 (x64) / 0x30000000 (x86) · console handoff via g_tty_console · seeded users: root / admin / user
programs: init.c · getty.c · login.c · vsh.c · netcat.c · libc.c · add one → new userlib/*.c + Makefile rules + mkfs_vernis.py --flag
Python host tooling (ai/) — build-time & test, runs on the developer machine
